question.label: Political conversations
question.name: Political conversations — what makes those moments hard to stay in?
question.description: Drills into the specific dynamic that makes him retreat from influence into execution.

answer: I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.

assessment.note: He names a specific gap in cross-org alignment, indicating a strong clarity deficit around how to operate in political stakeholder situations. [THREAD-DEFERRED] Concrete thread: 'cross-org alignment' hesitation not drilled because drill cap requires advancing the arc.

new_queue.label: Stretch now
new_queue.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?

new_queue.label: Current scope
new_queue.name: What's feeling most solid in your role right now, Ahmed?

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Next-level scope
new_queue.name: What does senior scope mean to you in practice?

new_queue.label: Trade-offs ahead
new_queue.name: What are you choosing not to own over the next six to twelve weeks?

new_queue.label: First move
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first?

history.answer_excerpt: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
